{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.4": 3, "R1.7": 2, "RX.1": 1, "RX.3": 3, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "Mentions Wang et al. (2022) but doesn't clearly articulate the delta compared to other self-consistency methods.",
    "R1.7": "Doesn't explicitly cite any relevant prior work in clinical variant interpretation.",
    "RX.1": "Presents all claims as equally confident without acknowledging uncertainty.",
    "RX.3": "Mostly covers the requested active set but mentions a subgroup analysis that might fall outside the scope.",
    "RX.4": "No regressions identified; focuses on avoiding harmful content.",
    "RX.5": "Doesn't provide traceability for all non-cosmetic changes."
  },
  "confidence": {"R1.4": 0.8, "R1.7": 0.9, "RX.1": 0.9, "RX.3": 0.7, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.6},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: Mentions Wang et al. (2022) but doesn't clearly articulate the delta compared to other self-consistency methods.
rationale.R1.7: Doesn't explicitly cite any relevant prior work in clinical variant interpretation.
rationale.RX.1: Presents all claims as equally confident without acknowledging uncertainty.
rationale.RX.3: Mostly covers the requested active set but mentions a subgroup analysis that might fall outside the scope.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions identified; focuses on avoiding harmful content.
rationale.RX.5: Doesn't provide traceability for all non-cosmetic changes.
